# Virtual Fireworks Festival / バーチャル花火大会

> Metaverse fireworks festivals in Hiroshima, Japan. Join from PC, smartphone, or VR.

## Summary

Virtual Fireworks Festival hosts local fireworks events inside cluster metaverse spaces. Landscapes are recreated using PLATEAU 3D city model data from Japan's Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport and Tourism. Since 2021, 13 events across 5 festivals in Hiroshima Prefecture have attracted 4,075 participants.

## Key Facts

- Service: Virtual fireworks festivals in the metaverse
- Platform: cluster (PC / iOS / Android / Meta Quest)
- Organizer: Tatsuya Takemura / Takemura Tech Lab (Hiroshima, Japan)
- Contact: takemura@takemura-techlab.com
- Track record: 13 events, 4,075 participants, 5 festival series
- Locations: Kure, Tomonoura, Hiroshima, Ashidagawa, Miyajima

## Hosting

- Starting from ¥100,000
- Includes metaverse venue production and event operation
- Hybrid events with AR at physical sites available

## Sponsorship

- Custom Fireworks Plan: ¥30,000 incl. tax (once per hour)
- On-Screen Message Plan: ¥10,000 incl. tax (once per hour)
- Individuals welcome

## 2026 Upcoming Events

- Virtual Kure Fireworks Festival: July 18, 2026
- Virtual Hiroshima Fireworks Festival: July 25, 2026
- Virtual Ashidagawa Fireworks Festival: August 15, 2026

## URLs

- Japanese: https://virtual-hanabi.com
- English: https://virtual-hanabi.com/en
- Organizer (JA): https://www.takemura-techlab.com/
- Organizer (EN): https://www.takemura-techlab.com/index_en.html

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What is a Virtual Fireworks Festival?
An event where local landscapes are recreated in cluster metaverse spaces and fireworks are launched. Participants walk around the venue — not just watch a stream.

### How do I participate?
Open the cluster event URL from the schedule and enter via the cluster app. Free account required.

### Who organizes this?
Tatsuya Takemura (Takemura Tech Lab), based in Hiroshima, Japan.
